title: Iran Declares Strait of Hormuz 'Completely Open'; Trump Maintains Naval Blockade
detail: Iran's foreign minister declared the Strait of Hormuz 'completely open' to all commercial shipping on April 17, 2026 — prompting a sharp 10%+ drop in global oil prices. Trump acknowledged Iran's announcement, posting 'Thank you' on Truth Social, but immediately clarified that the US Navy blockade 'will remain in full force' until a comprehensive long-term peace agreement is reached. Trump hinted at a broader deal framework and reportedly set an April 22 deadline coinciding with the ceasefire expiration. Iran's state TV characterized the opening as under 'strict management and control' by Iranian military, with restrictions on ships linked to hostile nations — creating ambiguity about the actual scope of re-opening.

media.caption: Iran declares Hormuz open; Trump maintains blockade pending comprehensive deal

title: House Breaks with Trump: 4 Republicans Vote Against Ending Haitian TPS Deportation Protections
detail: The US House of Representatives voted against the Trump administration's effort to end Temporary Protected Status (TPS) for Haitian nationals. Four Republican representatives — Maria Elvira Salazar (FL), Brian Fitzpatrick (PA), Mike Lawler (NY), and Don Bacon (NE) — joined all Democrats in backing a discharge petition to block the administration from stripping deportation protections from Haitians. The vote represents a rare congressional check on Trump's immigration enforcement agenda, driven largely by constituents with Haitian-American communities in those districts.

media.caption: House breaks with Trump on Haitian deportation protections vote

title: Immigration Application Backlog Tops 2 Million, Leaving Millions at Deportation Risk
detail: NPR reported on April 17, 2026 that the Trump administration has allowed a critical backlog of 2 million+ immigration applications to accumulate — more than the total increase during all four years of Trump's first term combined. The logjam leaves millions of people who filed paperwork to remain legally in the US more vulnerable to deportation because their cases have not been adjudicated. ICE separately disclosed it deported 442,000 people in the first full fiscal year of Trump's second term. Human Rights First documented nearly 120 deportation flights from San Diego area airports alone over the past year.

media.caption: 2M+ immigration applications stuck in backlog under Trump — millions at deportation risk
